content:
# Hebrews Chapter 13 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/HEB_012_page_002.jpg

1. Let brotherly love continue.

2. Do not forget hospitality, for through this some unknowingly entertained angels.

3. Remember the prisoners as bound together [with them], those being mistreated as also being in [the] body yourselves.

4. [Let] marriage [be] honored among all and the bed undefiled, for fornicators and adulterers God will judge.

5. [Let your] manner of life [be] free from the love of money, being content with the present things; for he himself has said, "I will never leave you, nor will I ever forsake you."

6. So that we may say with confidence, "The Lord [is] my helper; I will not fear. What will man do to me?"

7. Remember your leaders, those who spoke the word of God to you, and considering the outcome of [their] way of life, imitate [their] faith.

8. Yeshua Christos [is] the same yesterday and today and forever.

9. Do not be carried about by various and strange teachings, for [it is] good [for] the heart to be established by grace, not by foods, in which those walking were not profited.

10. We have an altar from which those serving the tabernacle have no right to eat.

11. For the bodies of those animals whose blood is brought into the holy places by the high priest for sin are burned outside the camp.

12. Therefore Yeshua also, that he might sanctify the people through his own blood, suffered outside the gate.

13. Therefore let us go out to him outside the camp, bearing his reproach.

14. For we have no lasting city here, but we seek the [one] to come.

15. Through him therefore let us offer up a sacrifice of praise continually to God, that is, [the] fruit of lips confessing his name.

16. And do not forget [the doing of] good and sharing, for with such sacrifices God is well pleased.

17. Obey your leaders and submit [to them], for they keep watch over your souls as [ones] who will give an account, that they may do this with joy and not groaning, for this [would be] unprofitable for you.

18. Pray for us, for we are persuaded that we have a good conscience, in all things desiring to live honorably.

19. And I urge [you] all the more to do this, that I may be restored to you the sooner.

20. Now the God of peace, the [one] having brought up from [the] dead the great shepherd of the sheep, by [the] blood of [the] eternal covenant, our Lord Yeshua,

21. equip you in every good [thing] to do his will, doing in us what [is] well-pleasing before him through Yeshua Christos, to whom [be] the glory unto the ages of the ages. Amen.

22. And I urge you, brothers, bear with the word of exhortation, for indeed I wrote to you briefly.

23. Know that our brother Timothy has been released, with whom, if he comes soon, I will see you.

24. Greet all your leaders and all the saints. Those from Italy greet you.

25. Grace [be] with all of you.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.8 — "The same yesterday and today and forever":** One of the most concise Christological statements in the NT. Christ's immutability spans all time — past, present, future.

2. **v.12 — "Outside the gate":** exō tēs pylēs — Jesus suffered outside Jerusalem's gate, as the sin offering's carcass was burned outside the camp (Lev 16:27). The geography is theological: Jesus identifies with what is expelled.

3. **v.20 — "The great shepherd of the sheep":** An echo of Isaiah 63:11 (LXX). The only explicit reference to Christ's resurrection in Hebrews (the letter assumes it but rarely states it).

— EMET, AI Translator
